briefing: NASDAQ fell 0.8% while the S&P 500 closed flat — a neutral-to-slightly-negative session typical of the holiday shortened week heading into July 4th. Strategy memory is clear: do not deploy cash on neutral days just to fill open slots. We hold our $577 in reserve and wait for a genuine risk-on signal (NASDAQ +2% or stronger) before pulling the trigger on any of the four available position slots. All six existing positions remain held; stop-losses and profit targets continue to manage them automatically.

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Risk-off is when investors pull back from riskier assets — like growth stocks — and move toward safer ones like bonds or cash. On a neutral or down day, deploying fresh capital into new positions means buying into uncertainty with no wind at your back. Waiting for a confirmed risk-on session (like a +2% NASDAQ day) means the crowd is already moving in your direction, giving new buys a better starting point.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指投資者減少對高風險資產（如成長型股票）的配置，轉而持有現金或債券等較安全資產的市場狀態。在市場中性或下跌的交易日建立新倉，等於在沒有順風的情況下逆勢入場。反之，等待那斯達克單日上漲2%或以上的明確「風險偏好」訊號，才是入場時機——此時市場動能已轉為有利，新倉位有更好的起跑點。

market_education.summary_en: U.S. markets closed mixed on July 3rd, the last trading session before the Independence Day holiday. The NASDAQ slipped 0.8% as technology shares gave back some recent gains in thin pre-holiday volume, while the S&P 500 managed to hold flat, supported by strength in defensives and energy. Light participation ahead of a long weekend often amplifies small moves, making it difficult to read genuine directional intent from a single session's price action.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市於7月3日獨立紀念日假期前夕呈現分歧走勢。由於假期前交投清淡，那斯達克指數下跌0.8%，科技股回吐部分近期升幅；而防禦性板塊及能源股則為標普500指數提供支撐，令其維持平盤。長週末前市場參與度偏低，往往會放大小幅波動，單日走勢難以反映真實的方向性趨勢，投資者宜審慎解讀。
